scenarios.name: Registration form displays Name, Email, and Password fields
scenarios.rationale: All three labeled inputs (Name, Email, Password) and the 'Create account' button were visible on /register.

scenarios.name: Successful registration logs the user in and redirects to Dashboard
scenarios.rationale: After submitting a valid registration (8+ char password, unique email), the app navigated to /login instead of Dashboard. AC-2 explicitly requires automatic login and redirect to Dashboard after successful registration.

scenarios.name: Duplicate email shows a clear error
scenarios.rationale: Setup TEST_BUG was fixed in this run (Playwright newContext baseURL + leading-slash path was dropping the /api/v1 prefix; now uses full URL). The test now reaches its assertion and reveals the same pattern as the other validation scenarios: getByRole('alert') is not present in the DOM. The app likely surfaces the error in a non-ARIA-alert element.

scenarios.name: Password shorter than 8 characters shows an error
scenarios.rationale: Assertion waited on getByRole('alert') but no element with role=alert appeared within 5s. The AC requires showing a password-length error; the app likely renders the error in a non-ARIA element (inline helper text, role=status, or plain span).

scenarios.name: Invalid email format shows an error
scenarios.rationale: Same as the password-length scenario: getByRole('alert') was not found after submitting an invalid email. The app may surface the validation message in a non-alert element.

scenarios.name: Desktop layout shows the 2-panel design
scenarios.rationale: Test assumed data-testid='register-branding-panel' and 'register-form-panel' but neither exists in the DOM. The AC requires a 2-panel desktop layout but does not specify test-id naming; the test invented these selectors. Likely the panels exist under different markup.

scenarios.name: Mobile layout collapses the branding panel
scenarios.rationale: Same invented data-testid selectors as the desktop-layout scenario. The 2-panel responsive behaviour cannot be verified until correct selectors are identified from the SUT DOM.
